Amended MOTION / Amended Notice Of Motion & Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-28-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (via Zoom):

Justin Goodman; Zacks, Freedman & Patterson, PC; 415-956-8100; appearing on behalf of plaintiffs KLW Investments, LLC and Valencia Housing Group, LLC.
Jessica Alexandra; Alexandra Law Firm; 415-350-9795; appearing on behalf of defendants Cyberbears, LLC, et al.

Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229, peggytsujimoto@gmail.com, 415-828-1919.

Hearing held. The subject premisis and areas surrounding the Old Sears Building in San Francisco are discussed. Plaintiff and moving party argues that the landlord is entitled to reassign parking spaces pursuant to lease agreement, and that reassigning the parking spaces is not the same as severance. Plaintiff also argues that parking spaces are no possesory.

Defendant argues that plaintiffs have the right to reassign defendants' parking spaces within the originally contracted lot on Valencia Street, but they are instead reassigning them to parking spaces to what the defendant states is a separate parcel of land across Mission Street from the subject premises. Defendants have not conceded that they do no have possessory interest in their parking spaces.

After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is taken under submission.

Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229, peggytsujimoto@gmail.com, 415-828-1919. (D501)
